title: The Dong with a Luminous Nose by Edward Lear — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
When awful darkness and silence reign
Over the great Gromboolian plain,
Through the long, long wintry nights; --
When the angry breakers roar
As they beat on the rocky shore; --
When Storm-clouds brood on the towering heights
Of the Hills of the Chankly Bore: --
meaning: "The Dong with a Luminous Nose" by Edward Lear is a sad and deep and nature poem consisting of 110 lines. The poet explores themes of sad and deep and nature through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "When awful darkness and silence reign Over the great Gromboolian plain,...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Edward Lear's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
